title: Smoky Fluffy Australian Tomato Flatbread
description: A lowsodium, fluffy flatbread crowned with smoky roasted vine tomatoes, fresh basil, and a hint of lemon zest-a romantic Australian brunch or lunch delight.
instructions: In a small bowl, dissolve yeast in warm water with a pinch of sugar. Let sit 5 minutes until foamy.
In a large bowl, whisk flour and smoked paprika together.
Make a well in the center, add yeast mixture, olive oil, and balsamic vinegar. Stir until dough starts to form.
Transfer dough onto a floured surface and knead 8–10 minutes until smooth and elastic.
Place dough in an oiled bowl, cover, and let rise 60 minutes or until doubled.
Preheat oven to 425°F (220°C). Spread halved tomatoes on a sheet, drizzle with olive oil, sprinkle pepper, and roast 15 minutes until slightly charred.
Punch down dough, divide into 4 equal portions, and roll each into a 6‑inch circle.
Brush each flatbread with olive oil, top with roasted tomatoes, basil, and lemon zest.
Bake 12–15 minutes until edges are golden and the bread feels puffy.
Remove, let cool 5 minutes, slice, and serve warm.
